title: What’s a Threat? Gabbard Says It’s Up to Trump, on Iran and Elsewhere.
summary: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, was left to square the president’s comments about an imminent nuclear threat from Iran with a letter from one of her trusted aides.

title: For U.S., Unmet Expectations in Iran Fit a Familiar Pattern in the Region
summary: Iran’s military retaliation, along with the political defiance of its new leaders, evokes a decades-old pattern of unrealized goals for American interventions in the region.

title: U.S. Intelligence Saw No Change in Iran’s Missile Capabilities Before War
summary: On Wednesday, the director of national intelligence and C.I.A. director contradicted one of the justifications the Trump administration had given for its attacks on Iran.

title: Trump’s Planning for America’s 250th Emphasizes Religion’s Role in the Nation’s Founding
summary: A closed-door White House event included news about the National Garden of American Heroes and an emphasis on the role of religion in the founding.

title: NYC High School Student Freed After 10 Months in ICE Detention
summary: The arrest of Dylan Lopez Contreras was the first case of a public school student in the city being taken by federal agents last year. “I still can’t believe that I’m out,” he said.

title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
summary: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.

title: Fed Holds Rates Steady as War in Iran Upends the Economic Outlook
summary: Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, emphasized the high degree of uncertainty stemming from the conflict as he acknowledged the potential for surging energy prices to lift inflation and dent growth.

title: Powell Says He Will Remain as Fed Chair Until Successor Is Confirmed
summary: Jerome H. Powell, who leads the central bank, also said he would not leave the Fed until a criminal investigation into his handling of renovations was over.

title: The Cost of the A.I. Boom: A Trade Deficit the President Detests
summary: A recent surge of A.I.-related imports has become an impediment to the smaller trade deficit President Trump wants.

title: Following Trump, Republicans in Congress Propose to Ban Most Voting by Mail
summary: A restrictive voter I.D. bill under consideration in the Senate could severely limit mail-in voting. Conservatives are pressing to end the practice outright, taking aim at an option that is widely used by voters.

title: Stratton, Illinois Senate Primary Winner, Promises a ‘Fight’ With Trump
summary: Ms. Stratton, a Democrat whose viral campaign ad featured voters’ profane views of Mr. Trump, said her campaign’s aggressive messaging was resonating.

title: ‘Their Power Feels Like Mine’: A Dog Sled Racer Says Goodbye to Her Pack
summary: After 20 years of racing, I wanted to take my sled dogs back into the wilderness.

title: Palestinian Man Recounts Brutal Sexual Assault by Israeli Settlers
summary: The man said his attackers stripped him naked, beat him and zip-tied his genitalia, an account corroborated by family members and a rights activist who were also beaten.

title: Silicon Valley Musters Behind-the-Scenes Support for Anthropic
summary: Tech companies have been reluctant to directly confront Trump administration officials over their contract feud with the A.I. start-up.

title: Google Sits Pretty as A.I. Rivals Compete for Pentagon Favor
summary: The tech giant has been rebuilding its relationship with the Defense Department and is poised to benefit as it sidesteps competitors’ controversies.

title: The Tropical St. Patrick’s Day That Honors African History
summary: Montserrat treats the holiday as both a national celebration and a more somber milestone: a commemoration of a failed slave rebellion.

title: Pakistan to Pause Afghanistan Airstrikes for Eid
summary: At least 143 people were killed in a Pakistani airstrike that hit a drug rehabilitation facility in Kabul on Monday, according to a top U.N. official.

title: Carol Kitman, 96, Dies; Photographer Documented the Vindman Twins
summary: A chance encounter in Brooklyn led to a decades-long project following the boys’ lives, from childhood to national prominence as critics of President Trump.

title: How Trump Should Extricate Himself From His Iran Quagmire
summary: Declaring victory and ending the war might not be so easy.

title: Joe Kent’s Resignation Letter Is Dangerous Because It’s Half True
summary: Kent’s resignation letter is partly rooted in truth, even if it taps into old antisemitic tropes about occult Jewish control.

title: Revelations of Cesar Chavez’s Abuse Spur a Widespread Outcry
summary: Also, oil prices jump after airstrikes hit a crucial energy site. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: After Vowing to Overhaul 911 Response, Mamdani Takes Cautious First Step
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is creating a Mayor’s Office of Community Safety, a pared-down version of a major campaign pledge with a smaller budget than promised.

title: NASA’s Hubble Telescope Spots Comet K1 Exploding Into Fragments
summary: In a stroke of luck, astronomers saw the comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS)  break into four or five fragments in November after it passed close to the sun.

title: He Tried to Rob 6 Banks, Police Say. His Total Takings: $605.
summary: A man is accused of handing tellers notes, demanding money at six Chase branches in five days. In three of the attempts, he left empty-handed.
